title: trifolium campestre var. lagrangei
intro: Trifolium campestre var. lagrangei, also known as the small or red clover, is a perennial legume plant that is native to Europe and Western Asia. It is known for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antibacterial compounds, particularly isoflavones and saponins. The plant has various health benefits, including reducing menopausal symptoms, improving cardiovascular health, and supporting bone health, as well as exhibiting potential anticancer and antioxidant properties. Traditionally, the leaves and flowers of the plant are used in herbal preparations, such as teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are believed to provide relief from digestive issues, skin problems, and respiratory ailments.
